podcasts.title: 4 SEO Claude Skills Nobody Is Talking About (For Shopify)
podcasts.transcript: Four powerful SEO skills you can directly install into your cloud, connect to your shop, >> [music] >> and point them to the products you already sell. One fixes the product description, one fixes the alt text on your images, one fixes the hidden structure that AI search engines and Google are ranking for, and one fixes the meta description and meta title. Those four things decide whether Google and AI search engines skip your product or recommend your product. So, get them right and you'll be set up to be found. You don't need to be technical to install this. You don't need any external software except for the Shopify connection, and everything is free in this video. So, are these skills, and I will show you step-by-step how to install them, how to use them. So, let's dive straight into it. Now, let's test these skills step-by-step together. This is my current product. It's a really bad product details, generic information in here, and it has no alt text, it has no meta description, meta title. So, it's the perfect product when you just imported from your supplier. You don't have any information or you're not sure if the meta description and title are even set. You can just take this product. Let's go to cloud. Let's go to the co-work tab, and here we need to make sure we first have our Shopify store connected. So, [music] we hover over this plus, we hover over connectors, we add a connector, and here we can search for Shopify. And if you already installed it, you see the icon here, and if you haven't already, you need to press install. You give it access, and it will directly have a connection with your store. Then we go over to customize. We see personal plugins here. Click on the plus icon. We hover over create plugin, then upload plugin. And here you upload the file that you can download in the free resources. And once you've done that, you see these skills in here. So, the alt text description, meta, and scheme. So, once we have that, we can go back to the co-work tab, and we can say, "Okay, I want to optimize my product. It's called the girls dress. I want to run the SEO suite skills in sequence to optimize this product for SEO and AI search. And as we see, it runs each skill in sequence. So, first the description, then the meta, then [music] the alt text, and at the end the schema. So, we already have the description and the information in there. Now, it asks us, "How do we want to make these edits or do we want to review them or just push them live?" Now, let's for this demo push them all to live and see what these skills come up with themselves. And on the right side here, we see the progress of what Lot will do, what steps it will take, and the connectors it's working with. It identifies the keyword based on the information that is in the product. And if we do want to upgrade these skills with external softwares to get the real-time SEO volume, we can do that and I will show you at the end of this video how you can edit these skills and how you can add this functionality to it. Now, it asks us access to execute to update the product and we click allow for this task. As we see, all four skills are done. Now, it's verifying. As we see, it's updated the description, it updated the meta tags, and the alt text for the image, and as well as the schema. So, let's check it inside of Shopify. Product details, it's already better. And for the meta description and meta title, we go to the admin, scroll down, and we do see it is updated. So, that's perfect. And also the alt text, we see the alt text here is also updated. So, the skill run through all these four steps and also the structure. So, now that you saw how this product is updated, let me show you how these skills are structured and how you can adjust them and even upgrade them. So, let's start with the SEO description skill. This will [music] rewrite an existing Shopify description. So, what you already have inside of Shopify, it uses that as the input and then it will run through this skill. First, it will ask you a question, "Do you want to push it live or as draft?" Then the flow is it will find the product, it will extract the facts, so what's already there, the variants, the body, title, then it will identify the target keyword based on what's already in the product, so in the description or in the title. It will not use an external software to get the keyword volume, do keyword research. It is not in here. I will show you in a second how you can do that. Then it will write the description with the framework, the hook, body, benefits, and the FAQ. It will save it to Shopify, and then [music] it will confirm what's changed and where to see it. And here are the steps in more detail, what's the actual flow is, and then it mentions, "Okay, then run the SEO meta skill, then the alt tags, and then the schema." So, let's go to the meta skill now. So, this is actually what you see inside of Google when you search for kids dress summer. This is what you see. This is the meta title, and this is the meta description. So, you want this to be optimized and not generic because Shopify then sets it themselves, and it could impact the amount of people that click on this. So, you want this to be optimized, and that's what the skill is doing. So, first it will find the product, it will identify the details again, will check the keyword, and then it will write the meta copy. And it has really strict guidelines on the characters. We don't want to exceed the characters, but we also don't want to keep it too short because we do want to make use of these characters. So, we aim for 50 to 60 characters. We want the product in there, the key attribute or the audience, and finally the brand. So, your brand name. Here it will give an example, and for the meta description, we're aiming for 140 to 160 characters. Again, we want to make use of the characters, but we don't want to exceed. And again, here an example >> [music] >> and a framework on the best practices. Then it will save it using the graph QL functionality. You don't need to worry about that. That's already configured. And step six, it will verify and confirm. So, for the alt text, it will give a description of what is actually in this image. So, when people are looking on Google images for a specific image, you do want to have a description of the actual image. So, when people are searching for a [music] kids summer dress, you do want to have your image optimized for this. So, when people are searching for that and they see your product and they click on it, they go to your website. That's the whole thing behind an alt text. So, first of all, it finds the product again. It pulls all the images and it classifies the alt text. What [music] What do we currently have? Is it empty? Is it generic? Then it rewrites what it needs. And then it reads the image. What do I see [music] in this image? Cuz you can have multiple images. Let's say the front view first of a sunglass, then you have a close-up of the sunglass, and then you have a side view. So, every single image does need to have a specific alt text. You cannot just put in the same alt text for all the images in one product. So, it identifies what's actually in this image. It downloads the image. It reads it. And then step five, it will update it. Step six, it confirms what it's changed. And then it goes to the next skill. We already did the description. We did the meta. And now the last skill is the schema. So, this skill enriches it in schema.org. [music] That's a framework that AI search engines do really like. You need to be optimized for this in order to have AI agents crawl your website and get the information that they're searching for directly. So, you want to be optimized for this. You don't want an AI >> [music] >> bot to go to your website and to have to look for all the information because then it rejects it. It goes to the next website. [music] We do have the structure correct. First of all, it finds the product again. It extract the schema facts that Shopify already have. Then it builds the enrichment. So, product fields we already have in there, and optional the FAQ page if we already have that. As we see here in step two, the exact schema facts. What's also a best practice if you do have the brand in here, and if you do have a GTIN. So, an identifier per product. Every single product has a specific GTIN or an EAN code, and this allows, let's say ChatGPT to identify this specific product. And when the user asks to compare a product inside of ChatGPT, when it has that GTIN or that EAN code, it knows, "Okay, this product is the exact same product as this competitor and as this other shop." Keyword overview, keyword suggestion, related keywords, and keyword data.
